creator: diizzy@FreeBSD.org
text:
I tried this HBA/controller with a few drives I had available and it seems to perform fine apart from the enumeration issue.

As Alexander said it doesn't seem to affect boot time to a point where it's of concern. Regarding the ahcich.X "hack" wouldn't that potentially cause issues if you would add another controller depending on which one gets detected first?

I did some basic testing create a zpool mirror on the spinning rust HDDs (WD and Seagate), cloned the ports repo, created a 20Gb dummy file using dd mostly to see performance and afterwards run zfs scrub afterwards successfully.

The SSD showed decent performance, at bit lower compared to what the Pioneer (LiteON) says on paper but that might also be related to the performance of the SBC (SoC).

root@generic:/home/freebsd # diskinfo -ctv /dev/ada0
/dev/ada0
        512             # sectorsize
        240057409536    # mediasize in bytes (224G)
        468862128       # mediasize in sectors
        0               # stripesize
        0               # stripeoffset
        465141          # Cylinders according to firmware.
        16              # Heads according to firmware.
        63              # Sectors according to firmware.
        PIONEER APS-SL2 Series  # Disk descr.
        RCJY124820TA    # Disk ident.
        ahcich1         # Attachment
        Yes             # TRIM/UNMAP support
        0               # Rotation rate in RPM
        Not_Zoned       # Zone Mode

I/O command overhead:
        time to read 10MB block      0.032413 sec       =    0.002 msec/sector
        time to read 20480 sectors   3.099710 sec       =    0.151 msec/sector
        calculated command overhead                     =    0.150 msec/sector

Seek times:
        Full stroke:      250 iter in   0.076516 sec =    0.306 msec
        Half stroke:      250 iter in   0.123976 sec =    0.496 msec
        Quarter stroke:   500 iter in   0.190986 sec =    0.382 msec
        Short forward:    400 iter in   0.160824 sec =    0.402 msec
        Short backward:   400 iter in   0.164504 sec =    0.411 msec
        Seq outer:       2048 iter in   0.274889 sec =    0.134 msec
        Seq inner:       2048 iter in   0.252086 sec =    0.123 msec

Transfer rates:
        outside:       102400 kbytes in   0.383572 sec =   266964 kbytes/sec
        middle:        102400 kbytes in   0.367526 sec =   278620 kbytes/sec
        inside:        102400 kbytes in   0.363141 sec =   281984 kbytes/sec

If it's of any interest the drives are attached to SATA ports 2-4 and didn't notice any excessive system load or such.

Best regards,
Daniel
